parts.title: Transcript
parts.annotated_transcript_html:
Speaker 1: You now have some time to look at the questions on your screen. Now answer the questions on your screen. I know it's early, but do you know anything about the first assignment that we have to do?

Speaker 2: Yes, it's already up on the economics faculty notice board. There's a choice of essays up there. I think there are about 10 you can choose from. I can't remember any of the titles, though, apart from the one that I think I'm going to write about.

Speaker 1: What's that one, then?

Speaker 2: It's about the short-term future of third world economies.

Speaker 1: Oh, I don't like the sound of that. It seems very wide-ranging.

Speaker 2: It is, but remember that I spent some of the summer vacation traveling in Africa and Asia, so that made me more interested in the subject, and I've also got some first-hand ideas on the subject.

Speaker 1: Does the essay list mention how long the essays have to be and the deadlines?

Speaker 2: Yes, you remember that last year all the essays had to be 3,000 words in length? Well, that's gone up by 1,000 words.

Speaker 1: Oh, no, they'll take ages.

Speaker 2: I know. I was hoping that they'd stay at 3,000 words or only go up to 3,500, but no luck, I'm afraid. What about the deadline?

Speaker 1: The first one has to be in by the 30th October, and the second by the 30th November. It looks like one essay a month, like last year.

Speaker 2: Well, at least that hasn't changed. But as it's at the end of September now, that leaves only a month to get the first one done. I'm going to have to get on to that straight away.

Speaker 1: Yes, you'd better. If you want to talk about it, I'm going to be at the campus cafeteria at 1.30 for lunch. We could go over some questions.
